page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-192-metal is a bare-metal server configured with 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors. Operating at a clock speed of 4.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this dedicated hardware includes 1.5 TB (1536.0 GB) of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor features a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. This bare-metal instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU, PassMark CPU, and PassMark CPU extended instructions tests all ranking in the top 10% of tested servers. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read and write bandwidth, achieving 464.93 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 201.86 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Database and memory-bound workloads demonstrate high efficiency, with PassMark database operations and Redis SET operations achieving top-tier results. Single-core performance, however, is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average and strong categories, respectively.

Given its high core count, dedicated bare-metal execution, and substantial DDR5 memory bandwidth, this server is optimized for memory-intensive compute workloads, large-scale database operations, and high-throughput static web serving. The inclusion of AMX instructions also enables strong performance in CPU-based large language model (LLM) inference, particularly for prompt processing. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture eliminates virtualization overhead, maximizing resource density and performance-per-resource for enterprise-scale parallel processing, high-performance computing (HPC), and memory-bound database clusters.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-192-metal is an x86_64 bare-metal server featuring 192 vCPUs (96 physical cores) powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors running at 4.0 GHz. It is equipped with 1.5 TB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, though it lacks local storage and GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, alongside strong performance in CPU-based LLM inference utilizing Intel AMX instructions. Conversely, single-core performance remains average. This server is highly suited for memory-intensive compute workloads, large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, and parallel enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-192-metal is a bare-metal server with 192 vCPUs, 1.5 TB of DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-192-metal is a bare-metal server with 192 vCPUs and 1.5 TB DDR5 RAM, optimized for high-compute database workloads.
tagline: A high-core bare-metal server with DDR5 memory and advanced matrix extensions, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise computing and database workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors at 4.0 GHz
1.5 TB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory (8.0 GB per core)
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Bare-metal architecture with zero virtualization overhead
Best suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and parallel computing
